questions.text: A relation R in a set A is reflexive if:
questions.explanation: A relation R is reflexive if every element is related to itself, i.e., (a,a) ∈ R for all a ∈ A.

questions.text: A function f: A → B is one-one if:
options: Every element has a unique image
questions.explanation: A function is one-one (injective) if f(x1) = f(x2) implies x1 = x2, i.e., different elements map to different images.

questions.text: An equivalence relation is reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.
questions.explanation: An equivalence relation must satisfy all three properties: reflexive, symmetric, and transitive.

questions.text: If f: A → B and g: B → C, then gof: A → C is called the ________ of functions.
questions.explanation: The composition of functions f and g, denoted gof, is defined as (gof)(x) = g(f(x)).
